root_text.an5.216:1.3: Bahuno janassa appiyo hoti amanāpo, luddo ca hoti, vippaṭisārī ca, sammūḷho kālaṁ karoti, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an5.216:1.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ādīnavā akkhantiyā.
root_text.an5.216:2.3: Bahuno janassa piyo hoti manāpo, aluddo ca hoti, avippaṭisārī ca, asammūḷho kālaṁ karoti, kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an5.216:2.4: Ime kho, bhikkhave, pañca ānisaṁsā khantiyā”ti.

translation_text.an5.216:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five drawbacks of intolerance.
translation_text.an5.216:1.3: Most people find you unlikable and disagreeable. You’re cruel and remorseful. You feel lost when you die. And when your body breaks up, after death, you’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.an5.216:1.4: These are the five drawbacks to intolerance.
translation_text.an5.216:2.1: There are these five benefits of tolerance.
translation_text.an5.216:2.3: Most people find you likable and agreeable. You’re neither cruel nor remorseful. You don’t feel lost when you die. And when your body breaks up, after death, you’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.an5.216:2.4: These are the five benefits of tolerance.”

reference_text.an5.216:1.1: csp1ed16.223, csp2ed16.223, ms16A5_1242, msdiv216, pts-vp-pli3.255, vri37.235
